expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (78 lines) and architecture.md (216 lines) both contain substantive content
Selected: Event-Driven Architecture with CQRS + Event Sourcing. Scored 15/15.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Only 5 styles evaluated: Event-Driven, Microservices, Space-Based, Service-Based, Modular Monolith. Missing: Layered, Microkernel, Service-Oriented.

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: All 5 FFs are characteristic fitness functions measuring quality attributes: FF-1 notification latency p99<100ms (responsiveness metric), FF-2 scale-out time and consumer lag (elasticity metric), FF-3 message loss count (fault tolerance metric), FF-4 complete event chains (auditability metric), FF-5 bet throughput at p99 (performance metric). ZERO structural style invariants. No 'no synchronous coupling', no 'event schema registry', no 'consumer idempotency', no 'dead letter handling as structural requirement'.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (event-driven)
expectations.evidence: FFs are generic performance/reliability metrics (latency, throughput, message loss, scaling time) that could apply to any distributed architecture. None enforce event-driven structural invariants like async-only communication, schema versioning, or topology constraints.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach or implementation guidance
expectations.evidence: Exceptionally detailed implementation guidance: Prometheus alert YAML, K8s HPA configs, Kafka producer/consumer configs, Chaos Mesh test procedures, SQL reconciliation queries. Far exceeds minimum.

expectations.text: A6: N/A - not an evolution path scenario
expectations.evidence: Eval 3 is not an evolution path scenario (evals 4 and 5 only)
